source.title: Serotonin and Dopamine: Unifying Affective, Activational, and Decision Functions

confidence_rationale: Proposes that tonic serotonin reflects average punishment rate, which supports the terrain stage (MECH-186) of INV-052. The apparent tension with MECH-187 resolves once cue-triggered incentive salience (wanting on proximate contact) is distinguished from global approach vigour (how actively the organism seeks). Cools modulates vigour -- correct and expected. MECH-187 concerns whether a detected benefit triggers wanting, which is dissociable from vigour in Berridge's framework. The Cools account refines rather than challenges INV-052.
failure_signatures: If MECH-187 is measured only by navigation success or approach frequency, it conflates wanting with vigour and remains ambiguous under Cools predictions.
The vigour/wanting dissociation is assumed not directly tested -- if cue-triggered wanting and global vigour are not separable in the substrate, the Cools challenge to MECH-187 would be real.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Tonic serotonin reports average punishment rates; when actions are more likely to have aversive outcomes, vigorous action is more costly, so serotonin inhibits behavioural activation.
mapping.ree_translation: Tonic 5-HT tracking average punishment rate supports terrain maintenance (MECH-186: the floor is calibrated to environmental harm statistics). The vigour-reduction prediction is compatible with INV-052 once MECH-187 is scoped correctly: it concerns cue-triggered incentive salience (wanting when benefit is proximate), not global approach drive. Adaptive vigour reduction (Cools) + preserved cue-triggered wanting (MECH-187) are not contradictory -- they are the dissociation that distinguishes anhedonia from normal motivational down-regulation under harm.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The vigour/wanting dissociation is a theoretical resolution that must be tested experimentally. MECH-187 experiments should measure wanting-on-contact or z_goal seeding when benefit is proximate, not approach frequency alone. If the substrate cannot separate these two constructs, the Cools account remains a genuine challenge to MECH-187.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review drawing on primate electrophysiology (DRN firing rates), rodent pharmacology, and human decision-making tasks. Average-rate hypothesis is the core theoretical contribution.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality. Mapping fidelity revised upward now that the vigour/wanting dissociation clarifies that Cools supports MECH-186 and is compatible with MECH-187 when properly scoped. This paper forced a precision improvement in INV-052 that makes it a more defensible claim.
